$comment: DISCLAIMER: This reference data is for informational purposes only. It does NOT constitute legal or tax advice. Tax laws change frequently — always verify with current legislation and qualified professionals. Data current as of March 2026.
title: Gift & Lifetime Transfer Exemptions
description: Gift tax exemptions, annual exclusions, and lifetime transfer allowances by jurisdiction, including lookback periods for gifts made before death.

lookbackPeriod.description: Gifts made within 7 years of death are potentially exempt transfers (PETs) and may become chargeable. Gifts surviving 7 years are fully exempt. Taper relief applies to tax on gifts made 3–7 years before death.

exemptions.label: Annual Exemption
exemptions.description: Each person can give away £3,000 per tax year free of IHT. Unused exemption can be carried forward one year only.

exemptions.label: Small Gifts Exemption
exemptions.description: Any number of gifts of up to £250 per recipient per tax year are exempt, provided the recipient has not received any part of the annual exemption.

exemptions.label: Marriage/Civil Partnership Gifts
exemptions.description: Gifts in consideration of marriage or civil partnership. Amount depends on the donor's relationship to the recipient.

exemptions.label: Normal Expenditure out of Income
exemptions.description: Gifts that form part of the donor's normal expenditure, made out of income (not capital), and leave the donor with sufficient income to maintain their standard of living. No monetary limit — must satisfy all three conditions.
conditions: Donor retains sufficient income for standard of living

exemptions.label: Spouse/Civil Partner Exemption
exemptions.description: Gifts between spouses or civil partners are wholly exempt, provided both are UK-domiciled. Where the recipient is non-UK-domiciled, the exemption is limited to £325,000 (or they may elect to be treated as UK-domiciled).

exemptions.label: Charity Exemption
exemptions.description: Gifts to qualifying UK charities and registered community amateur sports clubs are wholly exempt.

exemptions.label: Gifts to Political Parties
exemptions.description: Gifts to qualifying political parties are exempt, subject to conditions on party qualification.

lookbackPeriod.description: There is no lookback period as such — all taxable gifts (those exceeding the annual exclusion) made during the donor's lifetime are cumulated and reduce the unified estate tax exemption. Gifts within 3 years of death are included in the gross estate for certain purposes (e.g., gift tax paid, life insurance transfers).

exemptions.label: Annual Gift Tax Exclusion
exemptions.description: Each donor can give up to $18,000 per recipient per year (2024) without incurring gift tax or reducing the lifetime exemption. Married couples can 'gift-split' to effectively give $36,000 per recipient.

exemptions.label: Lifetime Gift Tax Exemption
exemptions.description: Unified with the estate tax exemption. Taxable gifts (above the annual exclusion) reduce the available estate tax exemption dollar-for-dollar.

exemptions.label: Unlimited Marital Deduction
exemptions.description: Gifts between US-citizen spouses are fully exempt. For gifts to a non-citizen spouse, the annual exclusion is $185,000 (2024).

exemptions.label: Charitable Gift Deduction
exemptions.description: Gifts to qualifying charitable organisations are fully exempt from gift tax.

exemptions.label: Education Exclusion
exemptions.description: Payments made directly to a qualifying educational institution for tuition are exempt. Does not cover room, board, or books.
exemptions.condition: Payment must be made directly to the institution

exemptions.label: Medical Exclusion
exemptions.description: Payments made directly to a medical provider for someone's medical expenses are exempt.
exemptions.condition: Payment must be made directly to the provider

exemptions.label: 529 Plan Superfunding
exemptions.description: A donor may contribute up to 5 years' worth of annual exclusions ($90,000 in 2024) to a 529 education savings plan in a single year, treated as spread over 5 years for gift tax purposes.

AU.name: Australia

lookbackPeriod.description: No gift tax. However, gifts may be assessed under Centrelink's deprivation rules for social security purposes (5-year lookback, $10,000 per year or $30,000 over 5 years).

AU.description: Australia has no gift tax. Gifts are not subject to income tax for the recipient. The gifted asset's cost base transfers for CGT purposes.
AU.source: Social Security Act 1991 (Cth), s. 1123; Income Tax Assessment Act 1997 (Cth)

CA.name: Canada

lookbackPeriod.description: No gift tax. However, gifting an asset triggers a deemed disposition at fair market value, potentially creating a capital gain for the donor.

exemptions.label: Inter-spousal transfer rollover
exemptions.description: Gifts between spouses can be transferred at adjusted cost base (rollover), deferring any capital gain. Attribution rules apply — income from the gifted property may be attributed back to the donor.

CA.description: Canada has no gift tax per se, but the deemed disposition rules mean the donor may owe capital gains tax. The attribution rules are a key consideration for inter-spousal and minor child gifts.
CA.source: Income Tax Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. 1 (5th Supp.)), ss. 73, 74.1–74.5

lookbackPeriod.description: Gift allowances (abattements) are renewed every 15 years. Gifts made more than 15 years before the donor's death are not aggregated with the estate for succession tax purposes.

exemptions.label: Abattement per child
exemptions.description: €100,000 per child, renewable every 15 years.

exemptions.label: Dons exceptionnels de sommes d'argent
exemptions.description: Additional exemption for cash gifts to children, grandchildren, or great-grandchildren, provided the donor is under 80 and the recipient is over 18.

DE.name: Germany

lookbackPeriod.description: Gift tax allowances renew every 10 years. Gifts made more than 10 years before the donor's death are not aggregated with the estate.

DE.description: German gift tax (Schenkungsteuer) uses the same allowances and rates as inheritance tax. Strategic gifting every 10 years can significantly reduce the overall tax burden.

lookbackPeriod.description: No lookback period for gifts. Gifts are taxed in the recipient's hands under income tax if they exceed thresholds, but gifts from specified relatives are always exempt.

exemptions.label: Gifts from specified relatives
exemptions.description: Gifts from specified relatives (spouse, siblings, lineal ascendants/descendants, and certain in-laws) are fully exempt regardless of amount.

exemptions.label: General gift threshold
exemptions.description: Aggregate gifts from non-relatives below ₹50,000 in a financial year are exempt. If total gifts exceed ₹50,000, the entire amount is taxable.

exemptions.label: Gifts on occasion of marriage
exemptions.description: Gifts received on the occasion of marriage are fully exempt.

exemptions.label: Gifts by way of inheritance
exemptions.description: Property received by way of inheritance or under a will is exempt.

JP.name: Japan

lookbackPeriod.description: Gifts made within 7 years of death (extended from 3 years in 2024) are added back to the estate for inheritance tax purposes. Transitional rules apply: the 7-year period is phased in from 2024 to 2031.

exemptions.label: Annual gift tax exclusion
exemptions.description: Each recipient can receive up to ¥1,100,000 per year tax-free (aggregate from all donors).

exemptions.label: Education fund gift exemption
exemptions.description: One-off exemption for gifts to a child or grandchild under 30 for education expenses, via a designated trust account. Expires 31 March 2026.

exemptions.label: Marriage/childcare fund gift exemption
exemptions.description: One-off exemption for gifts to a child or grandchild aged 18–49 for marriage or childcare expenses. Expires 31 March 2025.

exemptions.label: Settlement taxation (相続時精算課税)
exemptions.description: Elective system for gifts from parents/grandparents (aged 60+) to children/grandchildren (aged 18+). Provides a lifetime exemption of ¥25,000,000 with excess taxed at 20%, but all gifts are added back to the estate on death. From 2024, includes an annual ¥1,100,000 exclusion within this system.

JP.source: Sōzokuzei-hō (相続税法), Articles 19, 21-9 to 21-18; Sozei Tokubetsu Sochi-hō (租税特別措置法)

SG.name: Singapore

lookbackPeriod.description: No estate duty or gift tax.

SG.description: Singapore has no gift tax, estate duty, or capital gains tax. Gifts are not taxable for either the donor or the recipient.

IE.name: Ireland

lookbackPeriod.description: All gifts and inheritances within the same group threshold are aggregated over the recipient's lifetime. There is no time-based lookback — the lifetime threshold is cumulative.

exemptions.label: Small Gift Exemption
exemptions.description: Each person can receive gifts up to €3,000 from each donor per year without it counting towards the lifetime threshold.

exemptions.label: Spouse/Civil Partner Exemption
exemptions.description: Gifts and inheritances between spouses or civil partners are fully exempt.

exemptions.label: Dwelling House Exemption
exemptions.description: Inheritance of a dwelling house may be exempt if the beneficiary occupied it as their principal private residence for 3 years before the inheritance and does not own another property.
conditions: Beneficiary lived in the property for 3 years prior
Beneficiary does not own another residential property
Beneficiary continues to live in the property for 6 years after

IE.source: Capital Acquisitions Tax Consolidation Act 2003, ss. 69, 70, 86

NZ.name: New Zealand

lookbackPeriod.description: No gift tax (abolished 2011) and no estate tax.

NZ.description: New Zealand has no gift duty (abolished 1 October 2011), no estate tax, and no general capital gains tax.
NZ.source: Taxation (Tax Administration and Remedial Matters) Act 2011
